message:
feat(runtime): add tracing spans and metrics to conformal predictor (bd-1q5.7)

Add 'conformal.predict' tracing span with calibration_set_size,
predicted_upper_bound_us, frame_budget_us, coverage_alpha, gate_triggered
fields. Add INFO log on gate-triggered strategy downgrade, DEBUG for
prediction intervals. Add metrics events for histogram
(conformal_prediction_interval_width_us) and counter
(conformal_gate_triggers_total) via tracing conventions. Two new tests
verify span emission and gate_triggered recording.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1q5.4","title":"Terminal lifecycle management & capability detection","description":"Terminal init/shutdown with RAII cleanup guarantee. Capability detection via terminfo + runtime probing (sixel, kitty graphics, true color, sync brackets). Raw/cooked mode transitions with typestate safety. Signal handling (SIGWINCH, SIGTSTP, SIGCONT). TRACING: span 'terminal.init' with fields: term_type, capabilities_detected, color_depth. span 'terminal.shutdown' with fields: cleanup_success, restore_duration_us. LOG: INFO for lifecycle events (init, shutdown, suspend, resume), WARN for missing capabilities, ERROR for cleanup failures. METRICS: gauge terminal_active{term_type=...}, counter terminal_resize_events_total.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:08.563067285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:23:19.235708210Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:23:19.235688914Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: TerminalSession RAII (terminal_session.rs), TerminalCapabilities detection (terminal_capabilities.rs, caps_probe.rs), capability_override.rs, raw/cooked mode transitions, signal handling","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["kernel","lifecycle","ring1","terminal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q5.5","title":"Sync bracket flicker-free completeness proof","description":"Formal proof that sync bracket usage (BSM mode 2026) guarantees no partial frames are displayed. The proof must cover: (1) bracket open always paired with close, (2) no escape sequences leak outside brackets, (3) fallback behavior when terminal doesn't support sync brackets. Degradation: fall back to cursor-hiding strategy with WARN log. TRACING: span 'render.sync_bracket' with fields: bracket_supported, fallback_used, frame_bytes. LOG: WARN when sync brackets unsupported and fallback engaged.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:08.664982088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:36:34.494960960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T15:36:29.705865298Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flicker-free","kernel","proof","ring1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1234,"issue_id":"bd-1q5.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TanLion: Implemented sync bracket flicker-free completeness proof. Added cursor-hiding fallback to Presenter when sync brackets unsupported (DECTCEM ?25l/?25h). Added tracing span render.sync_bracket with bracket_supported/fallback_used/frame_bytes fields. WARN log on fallback. 30 tests covering pairing, enclosure, fallback, multi-frame consistency, state machine, inline renderer, determinism, adversarial, and stress scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:36:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q5.6","title":"Deterministic rendering pipeline","description":"Given identical model state, produce byte-identical terminal output. Required for golden-test infrastructure. No randomness, no timestamp-dependent behavior in render path. Controlled clock injection for animations. TRACING: span 'render.frame' with fields: frame_number, model_hash, output_hash, render_duration_us. LOG: DEBUG per frame with checksums. METRICS: histogram render_frame_duration_us, counter render_frames_total.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:08.749322595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:58:02.689056346Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:58:02.689032892Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic","kernel","rendering","ring1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1205,"issue_id":"bd-1q5.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified: render pipeline is already deterministic. No rand/random in ftui-render, no HashMap iteration in render path, row-major scan in diff, delta engine in presenter. Golden checksum tests (BLAKE3) already verify byte-identical output with non-determinism detection. Instant::now() only in perf instrumentation, not affecting output.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:57:57Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1q5.7","title":"Conformal prediction for frame-time budget gating","description":"Distribution-free prediction intervals for frame render times using split conformal prediction (section 12.1). Calibration set from recent N frames. Nonconformity score: absolute residual. Coverage guarantee: 1-alpha with alpha configurable (default 0.05). When predicted frame time exceeds budget, switch to cheaper diff strategy. TRACING: span 'conformal.predict' with fields: calibration_set_size, predicted_upper_bound_us, frame_budget_us, coverage_alpha, gate_triggered. LOG: INFO when gate triggers strategy downgrade, DEBUG for prediction intervals. METRICS: histogram conformal_prediction_interval_width_us, counter conformal_gate_triggers_total.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:08.832863204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:10:27.697878908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformal","kernel","prediction","ring1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1q5.8","title":"Unit tests: Cell/Buffer/Frame model","description":"Unit tests covering: Cell creation with all Unicode categories (ASCII, CJK wide, combining marks, ZWJ sequences, emoji). Buffer resize (grow/shrink/zero). Frame double-buffer swap correctness. Dirty bit propagation. Coordinate newtype arithmetic overflow protection. LOGGING SPEC: Each test must use tracing_test::subscriber to capture spans and assert structured fields are present. Tests assert: cell.dirty field transitions, buffer.resize span emitted with old_size/new_size fields.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:38.633330872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:48.330745382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:48.330726677Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: 141 cell tests, 150 buffer tests, 83 frame tests covering all Unicode categories, resize, dirty bits, coordinate arithmetic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ring1","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q5.9","title":"Unit tests: Bayesian diff strategy engine","description":"Unit tests covering: Prior initialization Beta(1,1). Posterior update with known change fractions. Strategy selection determinism for same posterior. Expected-loss computation correctness (verify against hand-calculated values). Edge cases: zero changes, 100% changes, NaN/Inf protection. Evidence term serialization. LOGGING SPEC: Assert tracing spans diff.strategy_select emitted with correct fields. Assert DEBUG log emitted on strategy switch. Property-based: QuickCheck/proptest for posterior invariants (alpha>0, beta>0, alpha+beta monotonically increases).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:38.720463991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:48.815105535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:24:48.815086830Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: Bayesian diff strategy tests exist in ftui-render with proptest invariants (14 tests in proptest_diff_invariants.rs) plus 150+ buffer/diff tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bayesian","ring1","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1q5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1q5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qb","title":"Implement Image widget with protocol detection","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: IMAGE WIDGET

## Background
Image widget displays images in terminal using Kitty Graphics Protocol, iTerm2 inline images, or Sixel fallback. Requires protocol detection and graceful degradation.

## Technical Requirements
- Protocol detection:
  - Kitty Graphics Protocol (XTGETTCAP query)
  - iTerm2 inline images (TERM_PROGRAM check)
  - Sixel (DA2 response parsing)
  - Fallback: ASCII/Braille representation
- Image loading via image crate
- Resize/fit modes: Contain, Cover, Stretch, None
- Aspect ratio preservation
- Partial update for animated GIFs
- Memory management for large images

## Design Rationale
Image support is a premium feature gated behind 'image' flag. Protocol detection happens once at startup and caches result. Fallback ensures graceful degradation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol detection and caching
- [ ] Kitty Graphics Protocol implementation
- [ ] iTerm2 inline image implementation
- [ ] Sixel fallback (optional sub-feature)
- [ ] ASCII fallback when no protocol available
- [ ] Resize mode configuration
- [ ] Aspect ratio handling
- [ ] Feature gate: image

## Performance Budget
- Protocol detection: < 50ms (one-time)
- Image encode/transmit: proportional to size

## Location: ftui-extras crate (feature = "image")","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:04.942919291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:42:28.303322783Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:42:28.303239056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qb","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","title":"task(test): execute full release rehearsal E2E scripts with telemetry/logging signoff","description":"Background
- Release track had planning artifacts but no explicit full-system rehearsal gate.

Goal
- Add a release rehearsal task that runs end-to-end user workflows through staged rollout logic with strict telemetry validation.

Scope
- End-to-end scripts that exercise remote attach, scrollback, selection, search, accessibility modes, and fallback rendering.
- Deterministic logging requirements: JSONL trace segments, benchmark outputs, rollout checkpoint events, rollback trigger evidence.
- Produce signoff packet linking rehearsal evidence to final go/no-go checklist.

Why This Matters
- Converts readiness theory into demonstrated release confidence.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests cover core logic and edge cases for this scope.
- Deterministic E2E scripts validate real user workflows relevant to this scope.
- Structured logs include timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for replay/debugging.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are implemented for the scoped workflows and runnable in CI.
- Test execution emits detailed structured logs (JSONL) including timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context.
- At least one deliberate failure injection scenario is covered and produces actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:07.689025233Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:16.114270191Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","frankenterm","release","rollout","telemetry","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.836846289Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:07.689025233Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:12.275031304Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:12.654309045Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.047206507Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:16.114147492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.448502229Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): advanced host API parity (events, parser hooks, markers, runtime options)","description":"Background
- Existing beads cover remote mode, attach lifecycle, selection, accessibility, renderer, conformance, security, and SDK surface.
- One critical parity area is still implicit instead of explicit: host-facing advanced API parity that real xterm.js embedders rely on (event lifecycle, parser hooks, marker/decoration primitives, and runtime option mutation).

Goal
- Deliver a first-class FrankenTermJS advanced host API track that matches or exceeds xterm.js practical capabilities while preserving FrankenTerm principles (Rust-first determinism, bounded resources, and explicit capability flags).

Scope
- Typed event API with deterministic ordering and backpressure-safe listener lifecycle.
- Parser hook APIs for CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC with explicit policy and quotas.
- Marker and decoration primitives (including viewport anchors) suitable for terminal-integrated UX.
- Runtime option and theme mutation APIs with validation and deterministic reconfigure semantics.
- Migration-grade compatibility mapping and evidence.

Why This Matters
- Without explicit advanced host API parity, complex xterm.js integrators cannot migrate cleanly even if core rendering/perf is superior.
- Making this a first-class feature avoids hidden scope and prevents regressions in release readiness.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This feature track must ship with comprehensive unit-level API and lifecycle tests.
- This feature track must ship with deterministic browser E2E fixtures that validate integration behavior.
- Validation must emit structured logs sufficient for replay-grade debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Advanced host API parity scope is fully decomposed into implementation and validation tasks.
- Unit + E2E + structured-log quality gates are defined and release-blocking.
- Compatibility evidence is available for migration and release scorecards.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Advanced host API parity is implemented across event lifecycle APIs, parser hooks, marker/decoration primitives, and runtime option mutation/theming.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover API contracts, ordering/lifecycle invariants, resource policy enforcement, and regression edges.
- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate advanced API behavior across supported engines and degraded-capability scenarios.
- Validation emits structured logs (JSONL) with sequence IDs, hook traces, option diffs, correlation IDs, and failure context suitable for replay.
- Compatibility evidence is integrated into release scorecard and migration documentation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:05.474877520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:23:35.401969703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","markers","parity","parser","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:05.474877520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:06.263774517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:06.642577073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.037688755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","title":"task(api-events): define deterministic host event taxonomy and ordering contract","description":"Background
- Integrators need deterministic, typed lifecycle events comparable to xterm.js (`onData`, `onBinary`, `onResize`, render/selection/title/bell notifications) but with stricter ordering guarantees.

Goal
- Define a canonical FrankenTermJS host event taxonomy, ordering contract, and failure/backpressure behavior.

Scope
- Enumerate supported events and payload schemas at Rust/JS boundary.
- Specify ordering guarantees across feed/render/reply/selection paths.
- Define listener lifecycle, disposal, and bounded buffering policy.
- Specify structured diagnostic fields per event class.

Why This Matters
- Clear event contracts prevent integration drift and eliminate undocumented behavior dependencies.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Add unit tests for event schema validation and ordering invariants.
- Add E2E fixtures that assert event sequencing under resize, burst input, and mode transitions.
- Emit structured event logs with correlation IDs for replay.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event taxonomy + ordering contract is documented and implemented as enforceable tests.
- Deterministic E2E sequencing checks pass across target browsers.
- Structured logs include event type, sequence number, timestamp, and correlation context.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267446554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267428580Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic host-event taxonomy/ordering contract with Node+WASM E2E fixture and structured sequence logs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.436385548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","title":"task(api-events): implement typed event emitter lifecycle with bounded delivery semantics","description":"Background
- Event contract design must be realized with a performant boundary implementation that remains deterministic under load.

Goal
- Implement event emission and listener lifecycle APIs with bounded buffering and explicit drop/backpressure semantics.

Scope
- Implement typed event emitters and subscription handles in WASM/JS boundary.
- Enforce stable ordering from the contract under concurrent feed/render updates.
- Implement listener disposal and memory-safe cleanup guarantees.
- Instrument event pipeline with structured tracing fields.

Why This Matters
- Reliable event delivery is mandatory for production embeddings and analytics hooks.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests validate ordering, listener disposal, and bounded queue semantics.
- E2E tests validate event behavior in realistic browser embedding scenarios.
- Structured logs capture queue depth, drop counters, and sequence continuity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event emitter implementation satisfies contract invariants under stress.
- Browser E2E scenarios confirm deterministic behavior and no leaks after listener disposal.
- Structured logs are emitted for all event pipeline anomalies and steady-state telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:16:49.927504581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","observability","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.211291673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.822786045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","title":"task(parser): implement CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC hook APIs with quotas and capability gating","description":"Background
- Advanced terminal apps rely on parser hook extension points (CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC handlers) for domain-specific control flows.

Goal
- Implement explicit parser hook registration APIs with deterministic execution order, quotas, and policy controls.

Scope
- Provide registration/deregistration for supported control-sequence classes.
- Enforce ordering, fallback behavior, and error isolation.
- Add resource quotas/timeouts to prevent host abuse.
- Integrate capability flags so unsupported hooks fail predictably.

Why This Matters
- Parser hooks are a key xterm.js differentiator; we need parity without unbounded risk.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests cover hook registration lifecycle, ordering, and isolation/fallback behavior.
- E2E scenarios validate host-defined handlers against real terminal traffic.
- Structured logs capture hook invocation traces, latency, and policy rejections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook APIs support CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC classes with deterministic behavior.
- Resource policy enforcement is validated by tests, including adversarial cases.
- Structured logging provides replay-grade hook execution traces.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449790873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449767009Z","close_reason":"Completed parser hook registration/deregistration for CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC with deterministic ordering, capability gating, quotas/timeouts, panic isolation fallback, and structured replay trace events in ftui-extras parser.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.981452926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.593585954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","title":"task(markers): implement marker and decoration primitives with viewport-stable anchors","description":"Background
- Marker/decoration primitives are needed for IDE-like overlays, diagnostics, and workflow UX parity.

Goal
- Implement marker + decoration primitives tied to viewport/scrollback model with deterministic anchor behavior.

Scope
- Marker creation/lifecycle APIs with stable anchor semantics across resize and scrollback compaction.
- Decoration model (inline/line/range) with renderer-safe invalidation behavior.
- Integration points for selection/search overlays without race conditions.
- Structured diagnostics for marker/deco churn and invalidation.

Why This Matters
- Rich terminal UX requires first-class annotation primitives; this is a common migration blocker.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for anchor invariants under scroll, resize, wrap, and compaction.
- E2E tests for marker/deco behavior across interactive workflows.
- Structured logs track anchor moves, invalidations, and render synchronization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Marker/decoration APIs are stable and deterministic under viewport mutations.
- E2E parity scenarios validate overlay correctness under churn.
- Logging surfaces enough context to debug anchor drift or stale decorations.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:15.834348970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","markers","renderer","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.377193365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.777374566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.176457101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","title":"task(options): implement runtime option/theming mutation API with atomic validation","description":"Background
- Integrators need runtime option updates (theme, cursor, behavior flags, perf controls) without re-instantiation.

Goal
- Implement runtime option mutation/theming API with strict validation and deterministic hot-reconfigure behavior.

Scope
- Typed option schema with safe defaults and capability/profile gating.
- Atomic update semantics and rollback on invalid payloads.
- Renderer + engine synchronization for option changes.
- Structured diagnostics for applied/rejected updates.

Why This Matters
- Runtime reconfiguration is central to production embeddings and migration parity.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for option schema validation, atomic updates, and rollback behavior.
- E2E tests for live reconfigure across backends and accessibility modes.
- Structured logs record option diffs, validation outcomes, and applied capability profile.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runtime option API supports deterministic hot updates with explicit validation failures.
- Cross-backend E2E scenarios confirm consistent behavior after live reconfiguration.
- Structured logs provide complete option-change auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:16.295417175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","renderer","sdk","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.576838396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:11.370001954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.972463790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","title":"task(layout): implement deterministic fit-to-container and web-font metric lifecycle","description":"Background\n- xterm.js users expect reliable fit behavior plus font-loading integration without cursor drift or geometry jumps.\n- FrankenTermJS currently lacks an explicit bead for this parity surface.\n\nScope\n- Implement deterministic fit-to-container API with host callbacks/resize observation contract.\n- Define font-load invalidation pipeline: metric recompute, cache invalidation, patch regeneration boundaries, and throttling.\n- Handle DPR/zoom/container resizes without off-by-one geometry drift.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent resize/font-load event streams yield identical viewport + cursor geometry outcomes.\n- No unbounded resize queue growth under storm conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Fit API behavior is deterministic for fixed input streams (resize, font-load, DPR changes).\n- Unit tests cover metric recompute ordering, cache invalidation, and queue-throttling edge cases.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate resize storms, zoom transitions, and delayed web-font loads with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- Geometry drift, cursor misalignment, and stale-glyph artifacts are regression-gated in CI.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep metric and fit logic in deterministic Rust/WASM state; JS host should remain thin.\n- Explicitly document rounding/tie-break policies for fractional geometry to avoid browser divergence.\n- Add failure diagnostics for font-load race windows and resize burst coalescing decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651803885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651785019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fit","frankenterm","layout","renderer","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:44.295223338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:43.930817274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","title":"task(links): implement OSC-8 + URL-provider hyperlink detection and annotation pipeline","description":"Background\n- xterm.js web-links usage depends on both OSC-8 hyperlinks and plain-text URL detection/provider behavior.\n- FrankenTermJS needs deterministic hyperlink discovery and annotation across viewport + scrollback.\n\nScope\n- Preserve OSC-8 hyperlink metadata through parser/state/grid/scrollback paths.\n- Implement deterministic URL detection/provider API with explicit ordering and conflict policy versus OSC-8.\n- Expose hyperlink annotation data for hover, keyboard navigation, and secure activation layers.\n\nAcceptance\n- Hyperlink ranges remain stable under scroll, resize, and reflow operations.\n- Detection conflicts resolve deterministically with documented precedence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- OSC-8 and URL-provider detection semantics are deterministic and documented (precedence, overlap, invalidation).\n- Unit tests cover hyperlink range stability under unicode graphemes, wraps, scrollback trims, and viewport reflow.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate hover/focus annotation correctness with structured JSONL logs.\n- Differential checks versus xterm.js hyperlink fixtures are wired into CI for covered scenarios.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep link annotations in core deterministic state so selection/copy/search can consume the same canonical ranges.\n- Explicitly log overlap-resolution decisions (OSC-8 vs provider URL) for postmortem clarity.\n- Do not allow hyperlink metadata paths to introduce unbounded per-cell payload growth.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:15:14.197071725Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T15:15:14.197037651Z","close_reason":"Implemented  {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","title":"task(image): implement bounded inline-image protocol decoding and cell-anchored rendering","description":"Background\n- xterm.js image addon users rely on inline image protocols for modern terminal workflows.\n- FrankenTermJS needs first-party, policy-driven support with hard resource bounds.\n\nScope\n- Parse and validate selected inline image protocol envelopes (capability-gated).\n- Implement deterministic image decode/upload pipeline with strict memory/frame budgets and eviction policy.\n- Render cell-anchored images with correct scroll/resize/viewport behavior and fallback semantics when capabilities are unavailable.\n\nAcceptance\n- Image rendering remains deterministic for fixed inputs/capability profiles.\n- Decode/render paths enforce bounded memory and queue usage under adversarial input.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Protocol parsing/validation for supported inline-image formats is deterministic, capability-gated, and fuzz/regression tested.\n- Unit tests cover decode limits, eviction policy, anchor semantics, and resize/scroll interactions.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate image-heavy workloads and failure modes (oversized payloads, malformed metadata) with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- CI gates fail on budget regressions (memory/frame time) or semantic drift in image positioning behavior.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep all image protocol support explicitly capability-gated and policy-controlled by defaults.\n- Enforce hard decode/upload caps before allocation; fail deterministically with reason-coded diagnostics.\n- Ensure image rendering never bypasses one-writer/frame-budget invariants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374427753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374343726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","image","protocol","renderer","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.010551183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:24.645156728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.371523095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","title":"task(progress): implement deterministic progress signaling API and OSC-mapped semantics","description":"Background\n- xterm.js progress addon usage depends on exposing terminal-originated progress signals to host UI.\n- FrankenTermJS should provide a first-party deterministic progress event surface without addon coupling.\n\nScope\n- Define supported progress signal sources (including mapped escape-sequence forms) and normalization rules.\n- Expose typed progress events with ordering guarantees and bounded delivery semantics.\n- Integrate with host API docs and capability flags.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent input/replay streams produce identical progress event sequences.\n- Unsupported/prohibited signals fail deterministically with traceable diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Progress signal taxonomy and normalization rules are explicit, deterministic, and versioned.\n- Unit tests cover sequence ordering, dedupe/coalescing policy, invalid signal handling, and capability gating.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate host-visible progress lifecycle behavior with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- API contract tests ensure typed event payload stability and error semantics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep progress events in the same deterministic ordering model as other host events.\n- Do not leak unbounded high-frequency progress updates; enforce bounded delivery/coalescing policy.\n- Include trace IDs linking parsed escape input to emitted host events.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.228948627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","parser","progress","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.364874191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.787264441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:43.211698509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","title":"task(text-render): implement optional ligature/shaping path with deterministic fallback","description":"Background\n- Many xterm.js users depend on ligature rendering for code readability in modern fonts.\n- FrankenTermJS should support this as an explicit, optional capability without sacrificing deterministic core behavior.\n\nScope\n- Add optional ligature/shaping render path with stable feature flags and fallback to canonical grapheme rendering.\n- Define cache/invalidation behavior for shaped clusters and font changes.\n- Ensure selection/cursor/extraction semantics remain correct when ligature shaping is enabled.\n\nAcceptance\n- Enabling/disabling ligature mode produces predictable, documented rendering differences only.\n- Core editing/navigation semantics remain invariant across ligature settings.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Ligature/shaping mode is capability-gated, deterministic, and documented with explicit fallback semantics.\n- Unit tests cover shaping cache invalidation, cursor/selection boundary correctness, and copy/extract invariants under ligature mode.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate ligature-enabled workflows across representative fonts with structured JSONL logs.\n- Performance and memory impacts are benchmarked and gated to prevent regressions.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat ligatures as a rendering concern; canonical text storage and terminal semantics must remain unchanged.\n- Document and test the exact mapping between shaped glyphs and logical cell boundaries for interaction correctness.\n- Ensure fallback path is always available and deterministic when shaping prerequisites are missing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.598328717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ligatures","renderer","text","unicode","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.684144315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:00.938379165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.315825142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","title":"task(test): add addon-parity compatibility harness (unit + browser E2E + differential + structured logs)","description":"Background\n- Addon-parity work must be validated as rigorously as core terminal behavior to avoid regressions and migration surprises.\n\nScope\n- Add focused unit/property tests for fit, links, image, progress, ligature, and web-font  {"id":"bd-2vr05.15","title":"epic(frankenterm-js): deterministic rescale + TeX-class real-time typography at 60+ fps","description":"North Star\n- Eliminate remaining web rescale correctness bugs while delivering a world-class text layout engine (kerning, leading, line-break, and justification quality) that feels immediate at 60+ fps.\n\nFirst Principles\n- Correctness before cleverness: layout must be deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.\n- Text quality is objective-driven: spacing, breaks, and vertical rhythm are optimized with explicit cost functions, not ad-hoc heuristics.\n- Performance is budget-governed: every frame and memory path has measurable constraints and fallback tiers.\n- Graceful degradation is automatic: overload never causes catastrophic UX failure or semantic corruption.\n\nProgram Scope\n- Web rescale hardening (ResizeObserver/DPR/zoom/font-load race windows).\n- TeX-class paragraph layout core (incremental Knuth-Plass + hyphenation + justification).\n- Shaping/kerning pipeline integration (cluster-safe cursor/selection/copy semantics).\n- Adaptive quality controller under compute pressure (controller + telemetry + evidence).\n- Exhaustive validation (unit/property/fuzz/browser E2E + structured JSONL evidence).\n\nOutcomes\n- No geometry drift, stale glyph artifacts, or resize-induced interaction corruption.\n- High-quality justified text with stable, deterministic line breaks and spacing.\n- 60+ fps sustained on representative workloads with explicit degradation policy when budgets are violated.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Rescale correctness failures are reproducible via deterministic traces and closed by regression tests.\n- TeX-class layout quality features (kerning/leading/justification/line-break quality) ship behind explicit capability policy with deterministic fallback.\n- Performance budgets and degradation tiers are implemented, measured, and enforced by CI.\n- Comprehensive unit/property/fuzz tests and browser E2E scripts exist with high-signal JSONL logs and deterministic replay metadata.\n- Release evidence bundles prove correctness + quality + performance under normal and overload conditions.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- This epic intentionally fuses correctness, typography quality, and performance engineering into one system-level program.\n- Existing relevant bug/parity threads to cross-reference during implementation: bd-2enu3 and bd-2vr05.14.1.\n- Use evidence-ledger mindset: each algorithmic decision should expose rationale and measurable impact.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":7200,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.215451002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.667176306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","frankenterm","layout","performance","typography","wasm","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.215451002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
